words.name: Vue
words.definition: A progressive JavaScript framework for building user interfaces, version 3
descriptions: Vue.js is a progressive framework for building user interfaces.
Vue 3 introduced the Composition API, improved performance, and better TypeScript support.
It uses a component-based architecture similar to React but with a simpler learning curve.

words.name: React
words.definition: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces, particularly web applications
descriptions: React is a declarative, efficient, and flexible JavaScript library for building user interfaces.
It allows developers to create reusable UI components and manage application state efficiently.
React uses a virtual DOM to optimize rendering performance.

words.name: TypeScript
words.definition: A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript
descriptions: TypeScript adds static type definitions to JavaScript, improving code quality and maintainability.
It provides better IDE support, early error detection, and enhanced code documentation.
TypeScript code is transpiled to JavaScript, making it compatible with any JavaScript runtime.

words.name: NodeJS
words.definition: A JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine
descriptions: Node.js enables JavaScript to run on the server-side, outside of web browsers.
It uses an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it lightweight and efficient.
Node.js has a large ecosystem of packages available through npm (Node Package Manager).

words.name: NextJS
words.definition: A React framework for production with server-side rendering and static site generation
descriptions: Next.js is a React framework that enables server-side rendering and static site generation.
It provides features like automatic code splitting, optimized performance, and simplified deployment.
Next.js supports both static and dynamic routing, API routes, and image optimization.

words.name: NuxtJS
words.definition: A Vue.js framework for building modern web applications
descriptions: Nuxt.js is a framework built on top of Vue.js that simplifies the development of universal applications.
It provides server-side rendering, static site generation, and automatic code splitting.
Nuxt.js includes conventions and best practices to help developers build scalable applications.

words.name: NestJS
words.definition: A progressive Node.js framework for building efficient and scalable server-side applications
descriptions: NestJS is a framework for building efficient, reliable, and scalable server-side applications.
It uses TypeScript and is built with Express or Fastify under the hood.
NestJS provides a modular architecture inspired by Angular, making it easy to organize and maintain code.

words.name: PostgreSQL
words.definition: An advanced open-source relational database management system
descriptions: PostgreSQL is a powerful, open-source object-relational database system.
It supports SQL and JSON querying, ACID transactions, and has extensive features for data integrity.
PostgreSQL is known for its reliability, feature robustness, and performance.

words.name: Python
words.definition: A high-level, interpreted programming language known for its simplicity and readability
descriptions: Python is a versatile programming language used for web development, data science, AI, and automation.
It emphasizes code readability and simplicity, making it an excellent choice for beginners.
Python has a vast ecosystem of libraries and frameworks, such as Django, Flask, and NumPy.

words.name: Django
words.definition: A high-level Python web framework that encourages rapid development and clean design
descriptions: Django is a Python web framework that follows the 'batteries-included' philosophy.
It provides an admin interface, ORM, authentication, and many other features out of the box.
Django is designed to help developers build complex, database-driven websites quickly.

words.name: TemporalIO
words.definition: A durable execution platform for building reliable distributed applications
descriptions: Temporal is a workflow orchestration platform that makes it easy to build reliable applications.
It provides durable execution, allowing workflows to survive failures and retries automatically.
Temporal is particularly useful for microservices, background jobs, and long-running processes.

words.name: FastAPI
words.definition: A modern, fast web framework for building APIs with Python based on standard Python type hints
descriptions: FastAPI is a modern web framework for building APIs with Python 3.7+.
It's built on top of Starlette and Pydantic, providing high performance and automatic API documentation.
FastAPI supports async/await, automatic data validation, and OpenAPI schema generation.

words.name: Docker
words.definition: A platform for developing, shipping, and running applications using containerization
descriptions: Docker is a containerization platform that packages applications and their dependencies into containers.
Containers are lightweight, portable, and ensure consistent environments across different systems.
Docker simplifies deployment, scaling, and management of applications.

words.name: Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
words.definition: A suite of cloud computing services provided by Google
descriptions: Google Cloud Platform is a collection of cloud computing services that run on Google's infrastructure.
It provides services for computing, storage, databases, networking, machine learning, and more.
GCP competes with AWS and Azure in the cloud computing market.

words.name: Flutter
words.definition: A UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications for mobile, web, and desktop
descriptions: Flutter is Google's UI toolkit for building beautiful, natively compiled applications.
It allows developers to write one codebase that runs on iOS, Android, web, and desktop.
Flutter uses the Dart programming language and provides a rich set of pre-built widgets.

words.name: Dart
words.definition: A client-optimized programming language for fast apps on any platform
descriptions: Dart is a programming language developed by Google, primarily used for Flutter development.
It's designed for building mobile, desktop, server, and web applications.
Dart supports both just-in-time (JIT) and ahead-of-time (AOT) compilation.

words.name: Drift
words.definition: A reactive persistence library for Dart and Flutter, built on top of SQLite
descriptions: Drift is a reactive persistence library for Dart and Flutter applications.
It provides a type-safe, reactive API for working with SQLite databases.
Drift generates type-safe code from SQL queries and database schemas.

words.name: Keycloak
words.definition: An open-source identity and access management solution
descriptions: Keycloak is an open-source identity and access management solution.
It provides single sign-on (SSO), social login, user federation, and fine-grained authorization.
Keycloak supports standards like OpenID Connect, OAuth 2.0, and SAML.

words.name: Selenium
words.definition: A suite of tools for automating web browsers
descriptions: Selenium is an open-source tool suite for automating web browsers.
It's commonly used for web application testing, web scraping, and browser automation.
Selenium supports multiple programming languages and browsers.

words.name: Microsoft SQL Server
words.definition: A relational database management system developed by Microsoft
descriptions: Microsoft SQL Server is a relational database management system (RDBMS) developed by Microsoft.
It supports transaction processing, business intelligence, and analytics applications.
SQL Server runs on Windows and Linux and integrates with Microsoft's ecosystem.

words.name: Javascript
words.definition: A high-level, interpreted programming language that is one of the core technologies of the web
descriptions: JavaScript is a programming language that enables interactive web pages and is essential for web development.
It's supported by all modern web browsers and can be used for both client-side and server-side development.
JavaScript is dynamically typed and supports multiple programming paradigms.

words.name: Virtual Box
words.definition: A free and open-source hosted hypervisor for x86 virtualization
descriptions: VirtualBox is a virtualization software that allows you to run multiple operating systems on a single machine.
It's useful for testing, development, and running legacy applications.
VirtualBox supports Windows, Linux, macOS, and other guest operating systems.

words.name: MQTT
words.definition: A lightweight messaging protocol for IoT and low-bandwidth networks
descriptions: MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) is a lightweight messaging protocol.
It's designed for IoT devices and networks with limited bandwidth or unreliable connections.
MQTT uses a publish-subscribe messaging pattern and is widely used in IoT applications.

words.name: SAO
words.definition: Service-Oriented Architecture - a software design approach where services are provided to other components
descriptions: Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA) is a software design approach.
It structures applications as a collection of loosely coupled, interoperable services.
SOA enables better integration, reusability, and scalability of software systems.

words.name: Firebase
words.definition: A platform developed by Google for creating mobile and web applications
descriptions: Firebase is a platform that provides backend services for mobile and web applications.
It offers services like authentication, real-time database, cloud storage, and hosting.
Firebase eliminates the need for backend infrastructure, allowing developers to focus on frontend development.

words.name: React Native
words.definition: A framework for building native mobile applications using React
descriptions: React Native is a framework for building native mobile apps using React and JavaScript.
It allows developers to write one codebase that runs on both iOS and Android.
React Native provides access to native platform APIs and components.

words.name: Material UI
words.definition: A popular React UI framework that implements Google's Material Design
descriptions: Material-UI (MUI) is a React component library that implements Google's Material Design.
It provides a comprehensive set of pre-built components for building user interfaces.
Material-UI is customizable, accessible, and follows Material Design principles.

words.name: Flask
words.definition: A lightweight Python web framework for building web applications
descriptions: Flask is a micro web framework for Python that's lightweight and flexible.
It provides the essentials for web development without imposing too many dependencies.
Flask is easy to learn and is great for small to medium-sized applications.

words.name: HTML
words.definition: HyperText Markup Language - the standard markup language for creating web pages
descriptions: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard markup language for creating web pages.
It structures content on the web and is the foundation of all websites.
HTML uses tags to define elements like headings, paragraphs, links, and images.

words.name: CSS
words.definition: Cascading Style Sheets - a stylesheet language used for describing the presentation of HTML documents
descriptions: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) is used to style and layout web pages.
It controls the appearance of HTML elements, including colors, fonts, spacing, and layout.
CSS allows for responsive design and separation of content from presentation.

words.name: ReactJS
words.definition: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces (alternative name for React)
descriptions: ReactJS is another name for React, a JavaScript library for building user interfaces.
It's commonly used to build single-page applications and interactive web interfaces.
ReactJS uses a component-based architecture and virtual DOM for efficient rendering.

words.name: SQLServer
words.definition: Microsoft SQL Server - a relational database management system
descriptions: SQL Server is Microsoft's relational database management system.
It's used for storing and retrieving data for applications.
SQL Server supports T-SQL (Transact-SQL) and provides enterprise-level features.
